present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly wakes up at six, opens the curtains, and greets the day.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly hits the snooze button three times before he finally wakes up.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly woke up early and felt completely ready for anything.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly woke up at nine and asked if he had missed anything important.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will wake up before the alarm because she always does.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will wake up late unless someone physically removes the duvet.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has woken up at six o'clock every day for forty years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has woken up on the wrong side of the bed every morning this week.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly gets up straight away, does a stretch, and makes the bed.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly gets up, walks to the kitchen, and immediately forgets why he came.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly got up at six and had a full hour to herself before breakfast.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly got up in the night to get a glass of water and fell over the cat.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will get up early so she has time for her morning walk.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will get up when he is ready, which could be quite some time from now.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has always got up before everyone else in this household.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has never got up without being called at least twice.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has a shower, washes her hair, and gets dressed in ten minutes.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly has a shave, nicks himself twice, and blames the razor.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly had a long bath with lavender salts and felt absolutely marvellous.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly had a shower with the water far too hot and stepped out bright red.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will have a quick shower before the grandchildren arrive.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will have a shave if he can find his razor, which is in the kitchen.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has had a cup of tea waiting on the bedside table every single morning.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has had a cold shower this week because he keeps forgetting to check the boiler.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly gets dressed carefully and always looks completely put-together.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly gets dressed in the dark and wonders why his shirt is inside out.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly got dressed in her favourite blue coat and felt very smart indeed.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly got dressed in a hurry and arrived at breakfast wearing two different shoes.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will get dressed and be ready well before anyone else comes down.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will get dressed eventually, once he has found his glasses.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has always got dressed before coming down for breakfast.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has got dressed in the wrong trousers three times this month.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly makes a pot of tea and sets the table before anyone else comes down.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly makes coffee in the teapot and then wonders why it tastes so strange.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly made tea, toast, and a bowl of porridge all before seven o'clock.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly made breakfast once and has been talking about it ever since.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will make a cooked breakfast on Sunday as a special treat.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will make himself a cup of tea, forget it is there, and make another one.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has made tea for this family every single morning for thirty years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has made every cup of tea slightly wrong since the beginning of their marriage.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly eats a proper breakfast and reads the paper at the same time.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly skips breakfast and raids the biscuit tin at half past nine.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly ate her porridge slowly and enjoyed every single mouthful.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly ate the whole loaf making toast before anyone else came downstairs.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will eat a boiled egg and soldiers on Sunday morning.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will eat standing over the sink and insist it counts as a proper meal.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has eaten a proper breakfast every single morning without exception.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has eaten cereal dry and called it a lifestyle choice since January.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly checks her messages, replies to every one, and puts her phone away.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly checks his phone and accidentally likes a post from four years ago.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly checked the family chat and sent a dozen photographs of the garden.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly checked his messages and replied to the completely wrong conversation.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will check the weather before she decides what to wear for her walk.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will check his phone at the table even though everyone has asked him not to.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has never once checked her phone during a meal in her entire life.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has checked his phone upside down and still replied with great confidence.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly walks two miles before nine every morning, rain or shine.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly walks the dog as far as the gate and counts it as serious exercise.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly walked along the river path and counted fourteen different birds.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly walked into the open shed door and said absolutely nothing about it for a week.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will walk her favourite route past the old church this morning.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will walk in completely the wrong direction and discover a new village.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has walked this same path every single morning for twenty years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has walked past his own front gate twice this week without going in.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly always catches the eight-thirty without ever having to run for it.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly misses the bus he wants and catches the one going in the wrong direction.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly caught the early train and had a whole free hour to herself in town.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly caught the right bus but got off at completely the wrong stop.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will catch the bus to the market before it gets too crowded.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will miss his bus by thirty seconds and wait forty minutes for the next one.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has never missed a bus she was actually trying to catch.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has caught the wrong bus so many times he now knows several new towns very well.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly runs errands on Tuesday mornings and does them all in one trip.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly runs out of everything at once because he never checks before going shopping.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly ran errands for three neighbours as well as herself, quite cheerfully.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly ran out of milk, drove to the supermarket, and came home without milk.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will run a few errands and be back well before lunch.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will run out of patience in the queue and leave without the thing he came for.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has run errands for almost every family on this street at some point.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has run out of excuses not to do the hoovering.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly meets her neighbour Dorothy for coffee every single Tuesday.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly meets people he knows, calls them the wrong name, and carries on regardless.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly met a new family on the corner and invited them for tea that same afternoon.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly met Mrs Bubbly's friend for the twelfth time and asked her name again.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will meet her daughter at the café at half past ten.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will meet someone important today and have something on his chin.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has met every single person on this street by their full name.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has met Mrs Bubbly's sister eleven times and still calls her 'the tall one'.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly grabs a coffee with Dorothy and they put the entire world to rights.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly grabs a coffee, orders the wrong thing, and drinks it without mentioning it.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly grabbed a takeaway coffee and sat in the park for a lovely half hour.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly grabbed what he thought was his coffee and drank someone else's entirely.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will grab a coffee on her way to the market this morning.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will grab a biscuit from the tin and finish the whole packet by accident.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has grabbed a coffee with someone she loves every Tuesday this year.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has grabbed the wrong bag from the café twice this month already.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly drops the grandchildren off with their bags, their snacks, and a hug.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly drives away with the school bag still in the car after he drops them off.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly dropped little Rosie off at ballet and stayed to watch the whole class.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly dropped Oliver off at the wrong gate and circled the car park twice.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will drop the children off early so they can play before the bell.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will drop them off on time only because Mrs Bubbly reminded him four times.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has dropped these children off at school every week for three years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has dropped them at the wrong school on one truly memorable occasion.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly sorts out the post, the bills, and the ironing before noon.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly sorts out one drawer and considers the entire house completely organised.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly sorted out the spare room in one afternoon and made it look wonderful.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly sorted out his paperwork by putting it all in a bag and hiding the bag.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will sort out the garden cupboard this weekend without being asked.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will sort out the garage sometime this year. He says.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has sorted out more problems in this house than anyone will ever know.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has sorted out nothing and moved everything slightly to the left.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly tidies up the whole house before she sits down for a rest.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly tidies up his newspapers into a pile and sits directly on top of them.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly tidied up after the party and the kitchen looked better than before.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly tidied up by putting everything in the nearest available cupboard.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will tidy up the kitchen well before the visitors arrive.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will tidy up by asking where things go and then putting them somewhere else.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has tidied up every room in this house at least twice today already.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has tidied up twice in seven years and expects recognition for both occasions.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly cooks a proper hot lunch from scratch every single day.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly sets off the smoke alarm every time he cooks, completely without exception.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly cooked a full Sunday roast for eleven people and loved every minute.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly cooked pasta without any water and genuinely could not explain it.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will cook a big pot of soup for the whole family this Sunday.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will cook something entirely unrecognisable and be enormously proud of it.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has cooked thousands of meals in this kitchen over the years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has cooked one truly successful meal and brings it up at every opportunity.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has lunch at the table, properly, with a glass of water and a napkin.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly has lunch standing at the counter and calls it efficient.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly had lunch with her daughter and they talked for three marvellous hours.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly had lunch at one o'clock and was hungry again by half past one.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will have lunch with Dorothy at the new place on the high street.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will have lunch late because he had a biscuit at eleven and completely ruined his appetite.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has had lunch at the same table every day for thirty years and loves it.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has had lunch standing up, in the car, and once in the bathroom, all this week.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly deals with every bill, letter, and form the same day they arrive.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly deals with the post by putting it on the kitchen table and walking away.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly dealt with the insurance claim in one short and very efficient phone call.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly dealt with the complaint by agreeing with it and hoping it would disappear.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will deal with the council letter before the end of the week.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will deal with it later, which means Mrs Bubbly will deal with it tomorrow.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has dealt with every piece of paperwork in this house for thirty years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has dealt with approximately none of it and is deeply grateful for this arrangement.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly looks after the grandchildren every Wednesday with tremendous energy.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly looks after the grandchildren by letting them do whatever they like.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly looked after three children, baked biscuits, and did the shopping on Tuesday.

future.mr: Mr Bubbly will look after the children and they will all have ice cream before four o'clock.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has looked after this family with total devotion for fifty years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has looked after himself admirably and let everyone else handle the rest.

present.mr: Mr Bubbly picks up three things nobody asked for and forgets the one they actually needed.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly picked up a beautiful bunch of tulips from the market for almost nothing.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly picked up the wrong bag and brought home someone else's shopping entirely.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will pick up the grandchildren from school at half past three.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will pick up something from the reduced section and be very pleased with the saving.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has picked up a few things for the neighbours while she was already out.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has picked up four jars of the same jam because he forgets they are already there.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly sits in the garden with her book and a cup of something cold.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly sits in the garden, falls asleep in the chair, and denies it completely.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly sat in the afternoon sun and read forty pages of her novel.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly sat in the wet garden chair and only noticed when he stood up to come in.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will sit by the window and knit until the grandchildren arrive.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will sit down for a quick rest and be found there at six o'clock.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has sat in this particular spot every summer afternoon for years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has sat on the cat, the post, and the television remote all in one week.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly waters her roses gently and has a quiet word with each one.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly waters the garden and soaks himself more thoroughly than the plants.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly watered everything while singing to herself in the afternoon sunshine.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly watered the plastic pot plant for three full days before anyone mentioned it.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will water the tomatoes and check on the sweet peas this afternoon.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will water one thing and accidentally flood something else entirely.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has watered this garden with great care every single day this summer.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has watered himself with the hose twice and still considers the garden done.

present.mr: Mr Bubbly fixes one thing and immediately introduces three new problems.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will fix the broken step before anyone twists an ankle on it.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has fixed every practical problem in this house that truly mattered.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has fixed things in ways that require a professional to come and assess.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will bring flowers and something nice to drink to the supper.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has brought warmth to every single room she has ever walked into.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has brought chaos to three separate counties this summer alone.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has listened to this family with patience and great quiet love.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will prepare everything well before the guests ring the doorbell.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has prepared dinner for this family more times than she could ever count.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has prepared one successful dish. It is framed in the hallway.

perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has arrived at the right place on the right day but at the wrong time, always.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has poured tea for more people than she could ever possibly count.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has told the best stories at every table she has ever sat at.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has laughed every single day of her long and truly remarkable life.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has laughed in all the wrong silences and, fortunately, most of the right ones too.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will clear up after dinner and find it oddly satisfying.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has cleared up after every family meal without a single complaint.

perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has never quite caught up with anything and is perfectly content about it.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly set her alarm, put her book away, and was asleep in four minutes.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly set the alarm three times, changing it each time, and woke Mrs Bubbly in the process.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will set the alarm and get a full eight hours without any difficulty.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will set the alarm and then set it again because he does not trust himself.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has set her alarm at the same time every night for decades.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has set seven different alarms tonight, just to be safe, all for different times.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly thinks of three good things before she closes her eyes.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly thinks he turned the heating off. He did not.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly thought about her grandchildren and smiled quietly in the dark.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly thought he had locked the door and got up to check it five more times.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will think of tomorrow with warmth and quiet excitement.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will think too hard about whether he left the garage door open.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has thought kindly of every person she knows before closing her eyes.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has thought about going back downstairs and has actually done so four times.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly falls asleep within minutes, content and completely at peace.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly falls asleep mid-sentence and begins snoring before the full stop.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly fell asleep thinking about her grandchildren's laughter.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly fell asleep in his clothes and was certain he had been awake the whole time.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will fall asleep knowing she has had a completely full and happy day.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will fall asleep the moment his head touches the pillow and deny it at breakfast.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has fallen asleep peacefully every night this whole week.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has fallen asleep in the chair, the sofa, the bath, and once at the kitchen table.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly sleeps deeply and does not hear a single thing until morning.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly sleeps loudly and does not hear anything either, for entirely different reasons.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly slept a full eight hours and woke up feeling completely renewed.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly slept through the fire alarm, a thunderstorm, and the dog barking next door.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will sleep well because she fills every day with something worthwhile.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will sleep like a man with a clear conscience, which is remarkable.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has slept well every night because she carries nothing heavy with her.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has slept through more of his own life than he would ever care to know.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly dreams of gardens, dancing, and the faces of everyone she loves.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly dreams of finding his glasses and then losing them inside the dream.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly dreamed she was young again, laughing beside the sea.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly dreamed he was a famous television chef and woke up very hungry indeed.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will dream beautifully tonight, as she always does.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will dream something confusing and describe it at considerable length over breakfast.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has dreamed the most beautiful dreams her whole long life.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has dreamed the most peculiar things and woken the entire household telling them.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly shares everything she has without ever keeping count.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly shares stories that grow considerably longer with every single telling.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly shared her last piece of cake with a complete stranger at the bus stop.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly used someone else's umbrella without asking. They were already standing under it.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will share her time, her table, and her garden with anyone who needs them.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will share information nobody requested with enormous enthusiasm.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has shared decades of warmth, wisdom, and very good cake.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has shared the wrong Wi-Fi password with absolute confidence for three years.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly keeps every letter, drawing, and birthday card the grandchildren ever made her.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly keeps losing his phone in rooms he just came from.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly kept every single promise she ever made to every single person.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly kept the wrong receipts and threw every useful one away.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will keep smiling through every season, every difficulty, and every year.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will keep meaning to fix the shelf for at least another three years.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has kept this family together through every storm with great grace.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has kept every newspaper since 1987. They are all in the garage.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly gives without thinking and always has more than enough left.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly gives advice that nobody asked for with absolute and total confidence.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly gave her warmest coat to the neighbour she knew was cold.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly gave a twenty-minute speech at Christmas dinner and missed the soup course.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will give every grandchild exactly the gift they most wanted.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will give up halfway through the wrapping and use a bag instead.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has given everything to this family and has no regrets whatsoever.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has given away the good umbrella to someone he genuinely cannot remember.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly remembers every birthday, anniversary, and first day of term without a diary.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly remembers 1974 perfectly and has few strong feelings about this morning.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly remembered to call the plumber before it became a genuine crisis.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly remembered it was bin day on Thursday. It was Wednesday.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will remember this day as one of the very best ones of the whole year.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will remember tomorrow. Or possibly the day after. He is looking into it.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has remembered every act of kindness that anyone has ever shown her.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has remembered things that never happened and forgotten things that absolutely did.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly never forgets what truly matters, even when everything else is busy.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly forgets why he came into the room before he has made it fully through the door.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly forgot to rest because she was too busy helping everybody else instead.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly forgot to put the bins out for the third week running and was very surprised.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will not forget a single person who has been kind to her.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will forget everything he was just told by the time he reaches the kitchen.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has never forgotten a birthday, an anniversary, or a promise.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has forgotten why he came upstairs and has now done it four times this evening.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly looks forward to tomorrow the same way she looked forward to today.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly looks forward to his nap with more enthusiasm than anything else in his diary.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly looked forward to the concert for weeks and every minute was worth it.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly looked forward to the dinner party and then fell asleep during the main course.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will look forward to seeing her daughter next week.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will look forward to the weekend mainly for the lie-in.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has always looked forward to Mondays. She is entirely unique in this.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has looked forward to things more than he has ever actually done them.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly gets on with absolutely everyone she has ever met.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly gets on with things in his own way, at his own pace, on his own schedule.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly got on with the new neighbours immediately and invited them for tea.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly got on with the job and finished it slightly wrong, but very cheerfully.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will get on with the baking this afternoon while the children nap.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will get on with the garage at some point this year, he says.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has always got on with people in a way that seems entirely effortless.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has got on with things at a pace that others describe as 'his own'.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly carries on regardless, through every difficulty, with a straight back.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly carries on doing things the way he always has and sees no reason to change.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly carried on cheerfully after the roof leaked, the boiler broke, and the cat ate the cake.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly carried on talking right through the important announcement and missed the whole thing.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will carry on being exactly who she is, for which everyone is grateful.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will carry on as normal, which is a source of comfort and chaos in equal measure.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has carried on inspiring everyone around her without ever noticing she does it.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has carried on regardless and the results have been truly unforgettable.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly takes care of every person, plant, and animal in a ten-house radius.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly takes care of himself admirably and has delegated the rest to Mrs Bubbly.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly took care of her mother-in-law for three full years without a single complaint.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly took care of the garden for one afternoon and overwatered everything in it.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will take care of everyone who needs it. She will not be asked twice.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will take care of his knee if Mrs Bubbly reminds him every day without fail.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has taken care of this family with quiet and complete dedication.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has taken care of himself and, in doing so, most of Mrs Bubbly's patience.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly comes up with wonderful ideas for presents, meals, and surprises.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly comes up with excuses as naturally and fluently as most people breathe.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly came up with the perfect solution in three minutes over her morning tea.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly came up with a plan, which is more than usual, even if it did not quite work.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will come up with something lovely for the grandchildren's half term.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will come up with a very good reason not to sort out the garage this weekend.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has come up with the best ideas in this family for the past fifty years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has come up with creative explanations for where various things have gone.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly puts up with a great deal and does so with extraordinary grace.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly puts up with things that bother him by simply not noticing them at all.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly put up with a great deal this week and did not complain to a single soul.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly put up with the noise from next door by turning his television up louder.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will put up with whatever comes with her usual quiet good humour.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will put up with inconvenience only if it does not affect his chair or his tea.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has put up with more than anyone will ever know and loved every moment.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has put up with very little and complained about considerably more.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly looks back on her life and sees very little she would change.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly looks back on his life and remembers only the highlights, which is quite sensible.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly looked back on forty years of Christmases and felt incredibly lucky.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly looked back on the project, smiled warmly, and took full credit for it.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will look back on today and know it was one of the good ones.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will look back on this period as a time he was extremely busy resting.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has looked back on her whole life with nothing but warmth and gratitude.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has looked back on every decision he has made and stood firmly behind each one.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly loves this life, this family, and every single ordinary, beautiful day.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly loves Mrs Bubbly more than he will ever quite find the words to say.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly loved every single chapter of this long and improbable story.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly loved her from the very first day and has never once stopped, not even briefly.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will love this family for every single breath she has left to give.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will love Mrs Bubbly until he has forgotten everything else, which is soon.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has loved this ordinary, extraordinary life with her whole heart.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has loved Mrs Bubbly for fifty years and would do every bit of it again.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly lives every day as though it is a gift. Because it is.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly lives in a state of comfortable confusion and deep, uncomplicated happiness.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly lived a life so full that not a single ordinary day was wasted.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly lived alongside Mrs Bubbly and somehow that turned out to be the whole thing.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will live every remaining day with great joy and great purpose.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will live the rest of his days happily and lose his keys every single one of them.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has lived a life overflowing with love, laughter, and outstanding cake.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has lived a truly wonderful life. He just cannot always recall the details.
